LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-30-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOHN JONES ON BEHLAF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, AND ON BEHALF OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC'S REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION CLAIMS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED REQUEST TO DISMISS CLASS CLAIMS WITH PREJUDICE AS TO PLAINTIFF AND WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO CLASS MEMBERS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THIS DISMISSAL WILL NOT PREJUDICE CLASS MEMBERS. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(301/CVA)

Request For Dismissal Of Class Action Claims
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOHN JONES ON BEHLAF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, AND ON BEHALF OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC's Request For Dismissal Of Class Action Claims.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed request to dismiss class claims with prejudice as to Plaintiff and without prejudice as to class members is granted. The court finds that this dismissal will not prejudice class members.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)
